draft.introduction: Luke 12:22–34 contains Jesus’ teaching on anxiety and material possessions, using the imagery of ravens and lilies. It articulates a radical trust in divine provision over economic security.

translation.text: And he said to his disciples, ‘Therefore I tell you, do not be anxious about your life, what you will eat, nor about your body, what you will put on. For life is more than food, and the body more than clothing. Consider the ravens: they neither sow nor reap, yet God feeds them.’
translation.original_text: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ · διὰ τοῦτο λέγω ὑμῖν μὴ μεριμνᾶτε τῇ ψυχῇ τί φάγητε

translator_notes.note: Luke uses “ravens” (κόρακας) where Matthew has “birds of the air.” Ravens were considered unclean animals in Levitical law, making Jesus’ choice of example deliberately provocative.
